question_title: Will the NFL place Terrion Arnold on the Commissioner's Exempt List before July 12, 2026?
summary: On June 29, 2026 — within the resolution window (June 27–July 11) — the Detroit Lions waived/released Terrion Arnold outright rather than placing him on the Commissioner's Exempt List. This is confirmed by 8+ credible outlets (ESPN, Pro Football Rumors, Spotrac, CBS, NBC/PFT, Yahoo). Pro Football Rumors explicitly states he was NOT placed on the Exempt List. The fine print specifies that release before Exempt List placement resolves NO, and the Exempt List mechanism requires an active contract (now moot as he is an unsigned free agent). There is no coherent path to YES. Probability set near the floor to reserve only for source-misreport risk.

factors.description: Lions waived/released Arnold outright June 29, 2026, not Exempt List placement — confirmed by 8+ outlets; PFR explicitly notes he was NOT placed on the list.
Fine print: release before Exempt List placement resolves NO. Release occurred June 29 within the window with no prior placement.
Exempt List mechanism moot once released (requires active contract); he is now an unsigned free agent.

reasoning:
**Forecast: 2% (NO)**

The question is effectively already resolved NO. On June 29, 2026 — within the resolution window — the Detroit Lions **released Terrion Arnold outright** rather than placing him on the Commissioner's Exempt List. This is confirmed by 8+ credible sources (ESPN, Pro Football Rumors, Spotrac, CBS, NBC/PFT, Yahoo), with Pro Football Rumors explicitly noting he was *not* placed on the Exempt List.

Per the fine print, a release before Exempt List placement resolves NO, and the Exempt List mechanism is moot once a player is released (it requires an active contract). Arnold is now an unsigned free agent. The 2% reserves only for source-misreport risk.
resolution_criteria: This question resolves YES if, between June 27, 2026 and July 11, 2026 (inclusive), the NFL formally places Detroit Lions cornerback Terrion Arnold on the Commissioner's Exempt List, as reflected on the NFL's official transactions page under the "Reserve List" filter (https://www.nfl.com/transactions/) or as reported by [credible sources](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions). Otherwise it resolves NO. A transaction is counted by its effective/announced date as recorded by the league or reported by credible sources.
fine_print: "Commissioner's Exempt List" refers specifically to that designation under the NFL Personal Conduct Policy. Placement on any other reserve designation (e.g., Reserve/Suspended, the Non-Football Injury list, or release/waiver from the team) does NOT count toward YES. If Arnold is released, traded, retires, or otherwise leaves the Detroit Lions before being placed on the Exempt List, the question resolves NO unless an Exempt List placement is recorded before such a departure. The relevant date is the placement's effective or announced date; a placement effective on or after July 12, 2026 resolves NO even if announced earlier as a future move. If neither the NFL transactions page nor credible sources report an Exempt List placement during the window, the question resolves NO.

factors.description: Detroit Lions waived/released Terrion Arnold outright on June 29, 2026 (confirmed by ESPN, Pro Football Rumors, Spotrac, CBS, NBC), rather than placing him on the Exempt List. Pro Football Rumors explicitly notes he was NOT placed on the Exempt List.
Fine print states if Arnold is released before being placed on the Exempt List, the question resolves NO. He was released June 29, within the window, with no prior Exempt List placement.
Once released, the Exempt List mechanism is moot (requires player under contract). He is now an unsigned free agent.

input.anchor: Base rate for Exempt List placement of a charged player is uncertain, but this specific case is nearly resolved by the release event.
input.assessment: Multiple credible outlets confirm the Lions released Arnold on June 29, 2026, rather than placing him on the Commissioner's Exempt List. Per the fine print, release before Exempt List placement resolves NO. The mechanism is now moot since he's an unsigned free agent. This is essentially a definitional/near-resolved question. Only residual risk: a retroactive Exempt List placement is essentially impossible once released. Probability near floor.
input.key_uncertainties: Whether any credible source could report a retroactive Exempt List placement before the June 29 release — essentially none reported.
input.tool_audit: research() tool returned comprehensive, consistent findings from 8+ credible sources. No failures.
input.process_reflection: The research tool was highly effective for this near-resolved question, immediately surfacing the decisive fact (release rather than Exempt List placement). No friction.

revision_history.reviewer_assessment:
All three factors are grounded in the research trace with no hallucination.

Factor 1 (Lions waived/released Arnold June 29, 2026; PFR explicitly notes he was NOT placed on the Exempt List): Directly supported. Trace quotes PFR "Arnold was not placed on the Commissioner's Exempt List; Detroit opted to release him outright" and Spotrac "Terrion Arnold (CB) Jun 29, 2026 - Waived by Detroit," corroborated by ESPN, NBC/PFT, CBS, Yahoo, Pride of Detroit, Heavy.

Factor 2 (fine print: release before Exempt List placement resolves NO): Correct reading of the resolution fine print; release occurred June 29, within the June 27–July 11 window. Strong resolution alignment.

Factor 3 (Exempt List mechanism moot once released — requires active contract): Trace states "the Commissioner's Exempt List mechanism (which requires a player be under contract) is moot." Grounded.

Logit signs all correctly negative, matching descriptions.

Pre-publication event: The release (June 29) predates published_at (July 1), but the resolution criteria set an explicit start window of June 27, so the event legitimately falls within range. Critically, the pre-publication event supports NO (the resolving-NO outcome), not YES — so the Case 2 concern (a pre-publication event manufacturing a YES) does not apply.

Double-counting: Factors 1–3 all lean on the single release event, expressed as three logical steps. Mildly redundant stacked logits, but not material — the release event alone settles the question and the aggregate points to the same near-floor answer the evidence overwhelmingly supports.

Resolution criteria: Provided in full; factors engage the specific fine-print terms (release-before-placement clause, window dates, Exempt List definition). No recovery attempt needed.

Tool audit: Agent claims "8+ sources, no failures." Ground truth is 6 calls, 0 errors — consistent. Research answer cites 8 distinct outlets.

Probability: My independent estimate is ~2%. Arnold was released June 29 with no Exempt List placement; the only YES path requires the Lions rescinding the waiver and recording an Exempt List transaction for a released (contractless) player — practically impossible. The 2% floor correctly reserves for source-misreport risk. Agent's estimate matches mine.

Counterargument is a genuine alternative story (waiver reversal / retroactive transaction) with an explanation of why it fails, not a token gesture. Confidence 0.96 is well-calibrated given 8+ consistent independent sources plus a decisive fine-print rule.

No errors found.
